changes.citation: rules.py:131-145 (_build_biomarker_rule); scoring_policy_registry.py:69-78 (_validate_policy bands block); rules.py:154-156 (silent skip); engine.py:192 (system iteration)

changes.finding: CONFIRMED. scoring_policy_registry.py:69-70: 'if policy.get("scoring_type") != "range_position": raise ValueError(...)'. This is a hard rejection of any scoring_type value other than 'range_position'. Lines 74-78: the six-band block iterates ['optimal','normal','borderline','high','very_high','critical'] and calls _validate_range_dict() for each — unconditionally. Any biomarker entry with a different scoring_type OR missing bands fails validation at module import, preventing the engine from starting. Sprint must extend this validator.

CONFIRMED. rules.py:133: 'bands = item.get("bands", {})'. Lines 135-140 unconditionally call self._to_tuple(bands['optimal']), self._to_tuple(bands['normal']), etc. If bands is {} (empty, because a bandless policy entry has no bands key), all six accesses raise KeyError at rule-load time. Sprint must add a conditional branch for lab_range_only rules that constructs a different rule or a modified BiomarkerRule without requiring band tuples.

CONFIRMED. rules.py:84-96: BiomarkerRule dataclass has six non-optional Tuple[float,float] band fields (optimal_range, normal_range, borderline_range, high_range, very_high_range, critical_range) with no default values. These fields ARE referenced by _determine_score_range() (lines 565-578) and _calculate_range_score() (lines 580-598). HOWEVER: calculate_biomarker_score() (lines 361-436) does NOT call either method — they are confirmed dead in the live scoring path. Making bands Optional[Tuple[float,float]] = None is safe for the scoring path, but _determine_score_range() and _calculate_range_score() must be guarded to raise clearly if called on a bandless rule.

CONFIRMED. calculate_biomarker_score() lines 382-425 already implements a complete lab-range-only scoring path: prioritises input_reference_range, applies HbA1c unit harmonisation, runs directionality overrides, and fails closed at lines 424-425 with UNSCORED_REASON when no lab range and not a derived ratio. The runtime scoring primitive is correct. The gap is exclusively in the load-time path: schema validation + rule construction + BiomarkerRule fields.

CONFIRMED. rules.py:394-402: calculate_biomarker_score() calls get_biomarker_rule() and retrieves policy_unit from the rule. If policy_unit is non-empty AND value_unit is present, the coherence check fires. For bandless rules that ARE loaded into a system's biomarker list, this check will run at scoring time. Bandless policy entries MUST still declare 'unit' (non-empty string). The schema validator extension must enforce 'unit' as required for lab_range_only entries. A bandless rule with an empty unit field will silently skip the coherence check — technically safe but architecturally undesirable.

CONFIRMED. scoring_policy_registry.py:28: '_policy_cache: Optional[LoadedScoringPolicy] = None' is module-level. load_scoring_policy() (lines 184-204) returns cache after first call. Any test that instantiates ScoringRules() gets the production policy (loaded at module import via line 21: '_POLICY = load_scoring_policy()'). Tests exercising bandless rule loading with a fixture YAML MUST reset _policy_cache to None AND patch _policy_path() to point to the fixture before instantiating ScoringRules. Alternatively, the sprint may add a ScoringRules(policy=LoadedScoringPolicy(...)) injection path. The chosen approach must be implemented consistently across all new tests.

CONFIRMED. scoring_policy.yaml:32-35: hormonal system has 'biomarkers: []' and 'system_weight: 0.0'. No thyroid markers present in the production policy. Sprint must NOT add TSH, FT3, FT4, or any thyroid-specific entry to the production scoring_policy.yaml. Tests must use a separate test-only fixture YAML.

CONFIRMED. The sprint can implement and test the full bandless architecture without modifying the production scoring_policy.yaml. The schema validator extension and rule builder changes are in Python code; a test fixture YAML carries the bandless test entries. If any change to scoring_policy.yaml is made (e.g. schema_version bump), the final report must prove: no production thyroid markers added, no production thyroid bands added, no non-thyroid weights changed, no existing scores drifted.

CONFIRMED. Scoring policy has one authority source: backend/ssot/scoring_policy.yaml, loaded via backend/core/analytics/scoring_policy_registry.py. No duplicate SSOT scoring policy sources detected. test_scoring_rules.py and test_scoring_lab_range_only.py both load the production policy via ScoringRules().

CONFIRMED. Behavioural scope: (1) scoring_policy_registry.py — extend _validate_policy() to accept lab_range_only scoring_type; (2) rules.py — extend _build_biomarker_rule() to produce bandless BiomarkerRule when scoring_type == lab_range_only; (3) BiomarkerRule dataclass — make band fields Optional with None default. No change to calculate_biomarker_score() scoring logic, no change to directionality path, no change to HbA1c harmonisation, no change to DERIVED_RATIOS/DERIVED_RATIO_POLICY_BOUNDS. The new path is activated only when scoring_type == 'lab_range_only' is explicitly declared in a policy entry.

CONFIRMED. Regression targets: (a) backend/tests/unit/test_scoring_rules.py — all existing tests must pass unchanged; (b) backend/tests/test_scoring_lab_range_only.py — all existing tests must pass unchanged; (c) any existing scoring policy validation test (test_scoring_policy_registry.py, test_scoring_policy_not_hardcoded.py per ADR Files reviewed). Production band-based biomarker scoring must produce identical outputs before and after the sprint. Cursor must run the full test suite covering scoring policy loading, biomarker rule construction, and scoring behaviour before finishing.

CONFIRMED. Scope is confined to: backend/core/scoring/ (rules.py, possibly __init__.py), backend/core/analytics/scoring_policy_registry.py, backend/tests/ (new tests only), docs deliverables (P1-8_scoring_lab_range_engine.md, ADR-SCORING-LAB-RANGE-ONLY-BIOMARKER-RULES-1.md, BUILD_DELIVERABLE_REGISTER.md update). Prohibited files explicitly confirmed absent from scope: domain_score_assembler.py, domain_narrative_wave1.py, wave1_subsystem_evidence.py, persisted_replay_contract_v1.py, health_system_card_evidence.py, knowledge_bus/compiled/, frontend/, Gemini paths, fallback parsers, Knowledge Bus packages, Pass 3 source material.

CONFIRMED. Determinism risk is LOW for the existing scoring path. The new bandless path is strictly opt-in via explicit scoring_type declaration — no existing biomarker entry in scoring_policy.yaml uses lab_range_only, so no existing scoring behaviour is reachable via the new code path. The only determinism risk is if the schema validator or rule builder changes introduce a side effect on existing rule loading. Mitigation: existing test suite must pass without modification; Cursor must verify all existing band-based biomarker rules still construct and score identically.
changes.citation: backend/ssot/scoring_policy.yaml:52-274 (all existing biomarkers use scoring_type: range_position)

changes.finding: CONFIRMED. (1) Engine CAN be safely extended without broad rewrite — change is targeted to _validate_policy() and _build_biomarker_rule(). (2) No output drift in existing scoring systems — bandless path is opt-in, existing rules unchanged. (3) Schema expectations are clear enough to modify safely — _validate_policy() is well-structured. (4) Bandless lab-range scoring CAN be made explicit and governed via scoring_type field. (5) Tests CAN prove existing markers unchanged. (6) Implementation does NOT require thyroid scoring policy activation. (7) Implementation does NOT require domain assembler / DTO / compiled card changes. Sprint is executable.

CONFIRMED. ADR-THYROID-SCORING-LAB-RANGE-ARCHITECTURE-1 documents four specific blocker points in the current code. All four confirmed by direct source reading. The architectural gap is real and unresolved before this sprint. Not a no-op.

overall_verdict: HARDENED — All five front-matter fields confirmed. All prerequisites and first-authority documents present. Scoring engine source content read in full and cross-referenced against ADR citations — all four blocker citations confirmed accurate. Three CRITICAL implementation requirements surfaced: (1) scoring_policy_registry.py:69-78 must be extended to accept 'lab_range_only' as a valid scoring_type and skip the six-band block for that type; (2) rules.py:131-145 _build_biomarker_rule() must add a conditional branch for bandless rule construction; (3) BiomarkerRule band fields must become Optional[Tuple[float,float]] = None with guards on legacy band-dependent methods. Two HARDENING NOTES: (A) unit field is still mandatory for bandless rules — schema validator must enforce; (B) _policy_cache singleton requires explicit reset in test fixtures — must not be silently tested against production policy. No STOP conditions triggered. Existing scoring path is unaffected by the changes. Cursor may proceed under TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH governance.
